frameworks.name: selective readout / utilization vs encoding
frameworks.one_line: dissociates biased encoding of evidence from biased use of encoded evidence for behavior

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: sequential visual-spatial evidence samples integrated toward a source estimate

frameworks.name: deliberative cognitive control
frameworks.one_line: top-down control could counteract confirmation bias since contradictory evidence is still encoded

core_question: Does confirmation bias arise from biased sensory encoding in the brain or from biased utilization of encoded evidence for behavior?
key_assumptions: cortical population activity measured with MEG indexes evidence encoding
post-choice samples can be split by consistency with the prior judgment
behavioral weighting of evidence reflects readout from encoded representations
manipulations: mid-sequence prompt to commit to a more likely source category
consistency of subsequent evidence with the previously chosen category
key_findings: processing of subsequent evidence depends on its consistency with the prior choice
parietal-cortex evidence representations contribute more to the report when consistent with the prior choice
contradictory evidence is precisely encoded but has little impact on subsequent behavior
interpretations: confirmation bias arises from selective readout/utilization rather than biased encoding
preserved encoding of contradictory evidence leaves room for deliberative control to counter the bias
